edge_hypothesis: The MACD R:R curve on ETH/4h has 4 data points: 3.5:1→PF=1.347, 5:1→PF=1.353, 8:1→PF=1.442 (but this was on 1h, not 4h), 12:1→PF=1.302 (4h). There is a critical gap: we know 5:1 produces PF=1.353 on 4h, and 12:1 produces PF=1.302 on 4h. Where does the 4h curve PEAK? The 8:1 champion is on ETH/1h — it may not replicate on 4h. If the 4h curve actually peaks at 6:1 or 7:1 (not 8:1), then the optimal forward-test portfolio should use 6-7:1 R:R on 4h rather than 8:1. This spec maps three untested points in the 4h R:R curve: 6:1, 7:1, and 9:1. Combined with existing data (3.5:1→1.347, 5:1→1.353, 12:1→1.302), this gives us 6 data points on the 4h curve — enough to precisely identify the peak. The MACD signal (MACDh zero-cross + close vs EMA_50) is the highest-quality entry in the system. Even small PF improvements at the optimal R:R point translate to significant portfolio returns. All variants use 1.0x ATR tight stop (matching the 1h champion's formula) to test whether the tight-stop principle improves 4h performance. Previous 4h variants used 1.5x stops. Invalidation: (1) if ALL three variants produce PF < 1.35, the 4h curve is flat above 5:1 and the optimal 4h ratio is 5:1 (already known); (2) if PF increases from 6:1 to 9:1, the tight stop + extreme R:R principle works even better on 4h than expected — extend testing to 8:1 and 10:1.

variants.name: macd_tail_harvester_6to1_4h
variants.description: MACD zero-cross on ETH/4h with 1.0x ATR stop and 6.0x ATR TP (6:1 R:R). First untested R:R point above the 5:1 ACCEPT (PF=1.353). Uses the tight 1.0x stop from the 1h champion rather than the 1.5x stop used in previous 4h variants. Breakeven win rate: 14%. This answers: does the tight-stop formula improve 4h MACD, and where does the 4h R:R curve begin to rise above 1.353?
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (6.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 6:1. Needs 14% win rate to break even after fees.
R:R curve mapping: first point above 5:1 on 4h with tight stop.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 6.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend confirmation for MACD zero-cross direction
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — highest-quality entry signal in the system
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 6.0x TP — maps 4h R:R curve gap between 5:1 and 12:1

variants.name: macd_tail_harvester_7to1_4h
variants.description: MACD zero-cross on ETH/4h with 1.0x ATR stop and 7.0x ATR TP (7:1 R:R). Second interpolation point in the 4h R:R curve. If 6:1 shows improvement over 5:1 (PF=1.353), does 7:1 continue to rise? The MACD signal's strength is capturing momentum shifts — at 7:1 R:R, we're betting that ~14% of momentum shifts produce moves exceeding 7x ATR. On 4h crypto, this represents multi-day trend continuations. Breakeven win rate: 12.5%.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 7:1. Needs 12.5% win rate to break even after fees.
R:R curve mapping: second interpolation point on 4h.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — 4h R:R curve second interpolation point

variants.name: macd_tail_harvester_9to1_4h
variants.description: MACD zero-cross on ETH/4h with 1.0x ATR stop and 9.0x ATR TP (9:1 R:R). This maps the decline side of the 4h R:R curve. The 12:1 variant on 4h produced PF=1.302 — a decline from the 5:1 PF of 1.353. Where does the decline START? If 9:1 is still above 1.353, the 4h peak is at or near 9:1. If 9:1 is below 1.353, the decline begins between 7:1 and 9:1. Combined with the 6:1 and 7:1 variants, this gives us enough resolution to pinpoint the optimal 4h R:R. Breakeven win rate: 10%.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 9:1. Needs 10% win rate to break even after fees.
R:R curve mapping: locates decline onset between 5:1 and 12:1 on 4h.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 9.0x TP — maps 4h R:R curve decline boundary
